##NAMES=Espadaite
##RRUFFID=R250051
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_4_Ca_3_Mg_2_[AsO_3_(OH)]_2_[AsO_2_(OH)_2_]_10_(H_2_O)_6_·H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless blades forming fans, jumbled sprays and random intergrowths in association with camanchacaite (R250031), chinchorroite (R250032),  magnesiofluckite (R250052),  picaite (R250053) and ríosecoite (R250054). Type sample described by Kamp et al (2019)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250051
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_0.83_Mg_0.06_[box]_0.11_)_4_Ca_3.00_Mg_2.00_[AsO_3_OH]_1.80_[AsO_2_(OH)_2_]_10.20_(H_2_O)_6_·H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.3649 b: 22.181 c: 18.3292 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 5027.1 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
